content: Autonomous agents can evolve their long-term task performance by iteratively compressing and summarizing their own motion and task planning memories, retaining only high-impact decision patterns rather than storing every raw interaction trajectory. This adaptive memory compression acts as an agent-native form of curation that aligns long-term memory retention with the agent's shifting active learning goals.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
